Hacker News Confidence

Less annoying hacker news. (What is this?)

This site scrapes Hacker News once every 30 minutes, then sorts according to this formula, with upvotes as positive signals and comments as negative ones. In my experience, the best articles to read on HN are the ones with a high upvote-to-discussion ratio. Mostly because controversial pieces tend to produce a disproportionate number of comments compared to upvotes.


  1. Optimizing Top K in Postgres 23 upvotes, 0 comments
  2. Building a Procedural Hex Map with Wave Function Collapse 451 upvotes, 68 comments
  3. Two Years of Emacs Solo 159 upvotes, 32 comments
  4. Lotus 1-2-3 on the PC with DOS 18 upvotes, 1 comments
  5. An opinionated take on how to do important research that matters 112 upvotes, 25 comments
  6. JSLinux Now Supports x86_64 290 upvotes, 80 comments
  7. Show HN: Remotely use my guitar tuner 145 upvotes, 36 comments
  8. The first airplane fatality 78 upvotes, 19 comments
  9. Darkrealms BBS 76 upvotes, 19 comments
  10. Show HN: I Was Here – Draw on street view, others can find your drawings 9 upvotes, 0 comments
  11. No, it doesn't cost Anthropic $5k per Claude Code user 95 upvotes, 38 comments
  12. Notes on Baking at the South Pole 50 upvotes, 17 comments
  13. So you want to write an “app” (2025) 109 upvotes, 46 comments
  14. Graphing how the 10k* most common English words define each other 32 upvotes, 10 comments
  15. OpenAI is walking away from expanding its Stargate data center with Oracle 315 upvotes, 167 comments
  16. Show HN: The Mog Programming Language 138 upvotes, 67 comments
  17. Ireland shuts last coal plant, becomes 15th coal-free country in Europe (2025) 925 upvotes, 566 comments
  18. Amazon holds engineering meeting following AI-related outages 15 upvotes, 4 comments
  19. Learnings from paying artists royalties for AI-generated art 96 upvotes, 53 comments
  20. The “JVG algorithm” only wins on tiny numbers 51 upvotes, 25 comments
  21. RVA23 Ends Speculation's Monopoly in RISC-V CPUs 10 upvotes, 2 comments
  22. Launch HN: Terminal Use (YC W26) – Vercel for filesystem-based agents 98 upvotes, 66 comments
  23. Windows: Microsoft broke the only thing that mattered 51 upvotes, 32 comments
  24. Show HN: DenchClaw – Local CRM on Top of OpenClaw 111 upvotes, 95 comments
  25. DARPA’s new X-76 185 upvotes, 177 comments
  26. Is legal the same as legitimate: AI reimplementation and the erosion of copyleft 410 upvotes, 449 comments
  27. No leap second will be introduced at the end of June 2026 95 upvotes, 98 comments
  28. Florida judge rules red light camera tickets are unconstitutional 400 upvotes, 524 comments
  29. Claude Code, Claude Cowork and Codex #5 4 upvotes, 1 comments
  30. A useless infinite scroll experiment 9 upvotes, 6 comments

Last generated 2:30pm, 10 March 2026. Source here.
